them no penalty can condone an
offense. If the Brewery Drivers
union had existed in the days of
the early Christians that great
preacher of all preachers, the
apostle Paul, would never have
been known, for they would have
put him on the unfair list and or
dered him to leave the country.


Let me say, Mr.. Editor, that the
right of conversion should never
be denied anybody, neither should
the right of repentance ever be
abridged. And whoever denies
these rights is cruel enough TO
place a captive on the sands of the
seashore where the devil is running
down the beach on the one side and
the tide running in on the other.
and then laugh at the dying spasms
of their squeezed out victim.


All labor organizations are vol
untary associations. No persons is
required 10 belong to any one of
them unless he wishes to, and any
effort to blacklist, boycott or vic
timize a member who has paid the
penalty of his offenses will dis
courage those who know not when
their turn may come next.


The writer hereof has spent
many years in the labor move
ment and never before did | hear
of a labor union resorting to the
tactics of Hartman and the gen
eral board of railway managers
and all the other corporate en-
ployers who seek TO force labor to
worship at the shrine of capital as
the price of employment. until |
heard of the Brewery Drivers
union Of Spokane. J. II. Sovereign
